reviewNotes: Strong regional signal and a sharper story than general birth rates.
Avoid pairing with birth-rate or children-share outside Cartographer.
Very close to life expectancy and male adult mortality.
Playable only as an expert mortality puzzle.
Distinctive enough for expert play because male risk patterns matter.
Too correlated with other mortality maps for default Daily.
Good synthesis of youth and aging pressure.
Fair when distractors avoid pure fertility lookalikes.
Useful land-use practice map.
Less Daily-worthy than arable land or forest area because pasture broadens the definition.
Strong economy-structure map that is not a raw-total GDP quiz.
Avoid unfair pairing with agricultural employment.
Good physical-geography and farming-belt reasoning.
Works best with probes in deserts, dense deltas, and temperate crop regions.
Readable infrastructure map with clear development signal.
Do not pair closely with clean fuels, water access, or electricity on easier tiers.
Too close to fertility rate and children-share for Daily.
Still useful as a Practice comparison map for demographic transition.
Distinctive agricultural productivity pattern.
Excellent expert-style Daily when reveal explains crop mix caveat.
Too correlated with birth and fertility maps for default Daily.
Useful Practice anchor for age-structure learning.
A household-energy map with a real public-health story.
High income correlation requires careful distractors.
High-interest climate map with resource-economy outliers.
GHG per capita is acceptable only as a hard-tier close distractor.
Interesting for macro nerds but too abstract for default Daily.
Keep in Expert Practice only.
Crude death rate is easy to misread because age structure dominates.
Good lesson map, not a default Daily map yet.
Budget-share map can surprise players but does not equal quality.
Needs careful caveat in reveal.
Clear infrastructure map with strong geographic anchors.
Avoid same-day pairing with water access and sanitation.
Strong work-structure map that rewards reasoning about sector mix.
Do not pair with employment-services on easier tiers.
Nearly inverse of agricultural employment.
Useful in Practice, not Daily.
Close to trade share and imports share.
Playable only as a hard open-economy distinction.
Volatile but interesting for finance/resource-flow players.
Too one-year-spiky for default Daily.
Valuable gendered labor map.
Needs expert context because social norms and measurement vary.
Iconic demographic-transition map.
Close demographic distractors are acceptable only at hard tiers.
Useful contrast with mobile-first connectivity.
Coverage and infrastructure nuance make it expert-only.
Excellent map-led geography puzzle.
Strong contrast among humid tropics, boreal regions, islands, and arid states.
Familiar baseline map and useful anchor for other reveals.
Do not pair with PPP outside hard tiers.
Too close to nominal GDP per capita for default Daily.
Good Practice comparison for purchasing-power nuance.
Interesting broader climate footprint map.
Too close to CO2 per capita for Daily without a very deliberate hard-tier setup.
Good non-obvious health-system spending puzzle.
Reveal caveat prevents reading it as health quality.
Close to trade and exports share.
Keep as Expert Practice until open-economy distractors are hand-tuned.
Almost identical to under-5 mortality.
Practice only so players can learn the distinction.
Volatile macro stress map can be fun but one-year shocks dominate.
Expert-only until editorial notes are more country-specific.
Readable connectivity map with strong regional anchors.
High development correlation means income-style distractors need restraint.
Useful labor concept but less visually memorable than unemployment or agricultural employment.
Practice-only for now.
Classic health map with fair global anchors.
Avoid pairing with adult mortality as an easy-tier distractor.
Interesting because values can exceed 100 subscriptions per 100 people.
Distinct from internet users and fixed broadband.
Readable aging map and strong contrast to youth-heavy regions.
Do not pair with birth-rate as a low-tier distractor.
Interesting health-finance map but not intuitive enough for default Daily.
Keep for expert players who enjoy system-design clues.
Strong air-pollution geography with recognizable regional belts.
One of the cleanest Daily environmental maps.
Highly map-native and strategically probeable.
Small crowded countries make this satisfying.
Gross enrollment can exceed intuitive school attendance readings.
Coverage is thinner and map pattern needs editorial review.
Good hydro/grid-mix geography puzzle.
Keep distinct from total renewable energy consumption.
Traditional biomass caveat makes the map counterintuitive.
Expert-only until the reveal copy can carry the nuance.
Exact inverse of urban population creates filler rather than a new puzzle.
Retired in favor of urban population.
Clear basic-infrastructure story.
Avoid same-day pairing with electricity and sanitation when possible.
Stronger education signal than primary enrollment.
Coverage is just adequate, so keep missing-data caveat visible.
Good outlier map for hosting and finance hubs.
Too technical for default Daily.
Near-identical to vulnerable employment.
Needs editorial decision before returning to playable catalog.
Coverage is exactly at the floor and reference year is older than most catalog items.
Hold until fiscal-capacity copy and data caveat are reviewed.
Interesting higher-education map but uses an older selected year.
Expert-only until year freshness improves.
Good open-economy map with small-state and port-economy clues.
Exports/imports only acceptable as hard-tier close distractors.
Distinct disease-burden map that is not just GDP.
Good expert-style Daily with clear source/year.
Strong child-survival map and clearer than infant mortality for Daily.
Infant mortality is acceptable only as a hard-tier close distractor.
Readable labor-market map with non-obvious regional pockets.
Definition caveat helps distinguish it from poverty or non-work.
Core settlement geography map.
Retire rural inverse to keep this version from feeling duplicated.
Near-identical to self-employed workers and hard to explain quickly.
Retired until the labor catalog is hand-curated.
Readable demographic momentum map that differs from fertility because migration and deaths also matter.
Good Daily candidate if same-day selection avoids fertility and birth-rate lookalikes.
Interesting family-planning map, but survey coverage and married-women denominator make it harder for new players.
Keep in Practice until manual QA confirms it is not just an opaque fertility proxy.
Too close to overall contraceptive use for this catalog pass.
Retired rather than exposed so the first 100 candidates do not feel padded by duplicate family-planning maps.
Strong, understandable health-system map with clear high and low anchors.
Works for Daily when not paired with multiple child-mortality indicators.
Good complement to maternal mortality because it shows service reach rather than death risk.
Daily-worthy if coverage is recent enough and missing data is visible.
Food-security pattern is legible and meaningfully different from income alone.
Keep the modeled/capped-value caveat visible in reveal copy.
Strong chronic-nutrition signal with a clear development story.
Daily-worthy if survey-year coverage survives the generated data check.
Acute nutrition signal is valuable but harder to reason about from a silent map.
Use for filtered Practice until manual QA checks survey timing and visual stability.
Interesting because it breaks the usual poverty-health pattern.
Practice-only for now because the unit is familiar but the geography is less intuitive.
Routine vaccine coverage is concrete and readable.
DPT can be a close Cartographer distractor but should not share an easy Daily with this map.
Useful health-system practice map, but very close to measles immunization.
Hold out of Daily to avoid two vaccine maps feeling like duplicate content.
Doctor density gives a direct health-workforce signal.
Good Daily candidate if coverage is broad enough and not paired with hospital beds.
Distinctive expert map with health-system legacy patterns.
Too easy to overread as health quality, so keep it out of default Daily.
Basic literacy is understandable for new players.
Daily eligibility depends on the pipeline finding enough recent, non-uniform country data.
Useful comparison against adult literacy.
Practice-only because many countries cluster near universal literacy, making the map subtle.
Good education-capacity puzzle for stronger players.
Expert-only because players may confuse it with enrollment or education spending.
Economy-structure map with good resource and manufacturing clues.
Close sector-share distractors are fair only on harder tiers.
Useful Practice map, but often acts like the inverse of agriculture/industry structure.
Hold out of Daily until sector-share variety is manually tuned.
More specific and often more interesting than total industry share.
Daily-worthy as long as distractors avoid turning it into generic GDP structure.
Volatile one-year macro map that can be interesting for experts.
Not Daily material because rebound years and shocks can feel arbitrary.
Useful income comparison map but close to existing GDP per capita.
Practice-only to avoid padding Daily with near-identical income maps.
Good Practice comparison to GDP PPP.
Too similar to existing income maps for default Daily.
Interesting migration-money map with distinctive small-economy anchors.
Expert-only because the denominator and diaspora story require explanation.
High-interest but politically and contextually tricky map.
Expert-only because GDP-share spending is not raw military power.
Good innovation-system challenge if coverage passes.
Expert-only because the map is technical and likely sparse.
Interesting export-composition puzzle with non-obvious winners.
Expert-only because it depends on manufactured-export denominator, not total exports.
Adds a governance/society map that broadens the catalog beyond economics and health.
Good Daily candidate because the geography is not a simple income gradient.
Distinctive map with Gulf, small-state, and migration-hub anchors.
Daily-worthy because it teaches a different demographic pattern than fertility or aging.
Important topic, but raw counts can make neighboring crisis hosts and large countries dominate unfairly.
Needs manual QA or a rate-based companion before becoming playable.
Positive and negative raw values are hard to communicate with the current one-direction choropleth.
Needs a diverging-map treatment or special copy before gameplay exposure.
Accessible and fun map with strong destination and transport clues.
Daily-worthy as long as reveal explains raw arrivals rather than per-capita tourism.
Good specialist tourism-dependence map.
Expert-only because percent of exports is less intuitive than arrival counts.
Useful consumption-intensity map if the data is recent enough.
Pipeline freshness will decide whether this can actually remain Daily-eligible.
Broad per-person energy demand map with good resource and climate clues.
Daily-worthy only if selected year and coverage survive validation.
Good expert contrast against renewable electricity and total energy use.
Too technical for default Daily because it is total energy, not electricity mix.
Conservation-designation map broadens the environmental catalog.
Readable enough for Daily if the generated map has enough variation.
Interesting marine map, especially for island and coastal countries.
Practice-only because landlocked countries and zero-heavy values may need careful QA.
Good physical-geography plus population-density reasoning.
Daily-worthy when paired away from generic water-access maps.
Strong water-pressure idea, but values over 100 percent can surprise players.
Expert-only until reveal copy and legend behavior are reviewed.
Compelling coastal-exposure map with a distinct topic.
Expert-only because players need to reason about percent of people near sea level, not coastline length.
The requested code is a percent-of-production measure, not fertilizer applied per hectare.
Needs manual decision before exposing a likely unit trap to players.
Useful trend-style agriculture map that is different from land share.
Daily-worthy if reveal copy makes the index base clear.
Too close to food production index for this expansion pass.
Retired to avoid a padded-feeling agriculture catalog.
Interesting finance-pressure map for advanced players.
Expert-only because coverage and GNI denominator can make it feel abstract.

Batch 4 QA: island and tropical biodiversity signals make this fun for advanced geography players.
Expert-only because raw species-count maps need careful framing.
Batch 4 QA: useful biodiversity hotspot map that differs from protected-area shares.
Expert-only because it is a raw species count and can favor larger countries.
Batch 4 QA: strong plant-endemism and biodiversity pattern with excellent coverage.
Expert-only because the raw count and extreme outliers are advanced.
Batch 4 promotion: broad source-valid chronic-disease mortality map with a clear health-transition story.
Daily-worthy when not paired too closely with adult mortality or life-expectancy maps.
Batch 4 QA: distinct regional health pattern with adequate coverage and clear rate unit.
Expert-only because the topic is sensitive and should not appear in easy casual rounds.
Batch 4 QA: useful treatment-access map but narrower than broad health-system indicators.
Expert-only until HIV-related distractors and reveal wording are reviewed together.
Batch 4 promotion: broad health-service map with strong coverage and an understandable 0-100 index.
Daily-worthy if kept away from health-spending and life-expectancy distractors.
Batch 4 promotion: high-interest health/environment crossover with a strong spatial pattern.
Practice-only because mortality-rate maps need careful answer-choice framing.
Batch 4 QA: source-valid health-system outcome map with good coverage.
Expert-only because reporting, case mix, and disease burden can make interpretation subtle.
Batch 4 QA: useful surveillance/access map, but the modeled percentage can be unintuitive.
Expert-only until reveal text handles values over 100 percent clearly.
Batch 4 promotion: clean age-structure map with broad fresh coverage and an intuitive percent unit.
Daily-worthy when not paired with elderly share or children share in easy tiers.
Batch 4 promotion: broad education-investment map with clear percent-of-GNI framing.
Daily-worthy, but reveal copy should distinguish spending share from school outcomes.
Batch 4 promotion: current high-coverage composite with a readable development pattern.
Daily-worthy sparingly because it blends health and education into one index.
Batch 4 promotion: useful education component of human capital with strong coverage.
Practice-only because composite education indexes are less intuitive than enrollment or spending maps.
Batch 4 promotion: sustainability-and-savings map adds development depth beyond GDP or income.
Practice-only because adjusted-savings concepts are advanced.
Batch 4 promotion: meaningful financial-inclusion map for poorer adults with a clear percent unit.
Practice-only because survey-wave timing and financial-inclusion distractors need testing.
Batch 4 QA: useful youth financial-inclusion signal, but close to other account-ownership maps.
Expert-only until the financial-inclusion cluster is tuned.
Batch 4 promotion: strong trade-openness map with broad coverage and a clear percent-of-GDP unit.
Daily-worthy if answer choices distinguish goods trade from services and tourism.
Batch 4 promotion: fun tourism-dependence map with a clear service-export share unit.
Practice-only because it can overlap with island, small-economy, and tourism distractors.
Batch 4 QA: source-valid trade-partner map, but the concept is abstract for broad play.
Expert-only and avoid pairing with import-side high-income trade maps.
Batch 4 QA: useful counterpart to export-partner geography, but less intuitive than total trade openness.
Expert-only until trade-partner distractors are reviewed.
Batch 4 promotion: regional-integration map with good coverage and a distinct geography story.
Practice-only because partner-share maps are more abstract than sector shares.
Batch 4 promotion: tangible financial-infrastructure clue with just enough coverage to pass the source gate.
Practice-only until checked against bank-branch and account-ownership maps.
